message.reasoning:
We need to respond with JSON only, following rules. Kind think|tool_call|complete. Need to decide next action. We have frontier recommending expand_file priority=112. So we should perform a tool_call to expand_file with appropriate selector.

We need to refer to short ids. We have short id naming like F1? Actually short ids appear in graph frontiers: they said [F1] relation=... but they didn't provide explicit short ids for nodes? In graph relations they show [F1] etc. So short ids are like F1, maybe also S1 etc. The instruction says short ids are stable within this turn and look like F1, S3, D2, or R1. Likely they refer to frontier identifiers. In frontier they gave "Recommended frontier actions: - hydrate_source [F1] relation=- direction=- candidates=0 priority=120 via session.hydrate(...)". Also "expand_file [F1] priority=112 candidates=17". So short id F1 corresponds to some node? Probably the file artifact.rs? Or maybe the expansion of file symbols? The recommended action expands_file [F1] meaning expand_file operation on node with short id F1. We need to call session.expand_file with target selector that corresponds to that node. How to refer? Use short id? Probably we can use selector string like "F1"? But selector expects something like a node identifier maybe the logical_key? The tool contract says session.expand_file payload includes target selector and optional depth etc. So we should provide target selector that resolves to the file we want to expand. Probably the file itself is a node with logical_key maybe "file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". That may have short id? Not given. But frontier says expand_file [F1] relation=- direction=- candidates=17 priority=112 via session.expand_file(...). So we can call session.expand_file with selector that matches that node. Maybe we can use "file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". That is a logical_key for file node. That might be expressed as "file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". Use that as selector string.

Thus produce tool_call: tool_name "session.expand_file", payload with target_selector maybe "file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". Also include depth? Not required. So payload: {"target_selector":"file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs"}.

We need to output JSON with kind "tool_call". Also need acquired_fact_ids: list of fact ids we acquire? Not yet. readiness_state: "not_ready" maybe? Since we are about to call tool. Should be "not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". Since we haven't acquired evidence yet, readiness_state likely "not_ready". So produce JSON:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"<brief status>",
  "tool_call":{"tool_name":"session.expand_file","payload":{"target_selector":"file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs"}},
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"not_ready"
}

But need to include acquired_fact_ids maybe empty array. The schema requires "acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"] but can be omitted? It says return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. It says acquired_fact_ids must be an array with a fact id. But earlier says use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names. It also says "acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"] . So we need to provide at least one fact id. We haven't acquired any fact yet. Maybe we can include empty array? But format expects array with a fact id string. Could we include placeholder? Might need to include a fact id we will acquire after the tool call? Usually we would acquire facts after tool returns. Since we are about to call tool, we haven't acquired any facts yet, maybe we can leave it empty array [].

But spec says must be array of fact ids; maybe it's okay to have empty array? It says "acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"] but that is example. Could be dynamic. I'd output [].

Also readiness_state must be one of the listed strings. Use "not_ready". That's allowed.

Thus final JSON.

Make sure no extra fields. Provide exactly as required.

Let's craft:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Expanding file symbols for artifacts.rs",
  "tool_call":{
    "tool_name":"session.expand_file",
    "payload":{
      "target_selector":"file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs"
    }
  },
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"not_ready"
}

Check no extra whitespace? It's fine.

Return as JSON only, no markdown.
